source: WV DEP — Coal NPDES + 2016 303(d) impaired streams
scope: active coal-mine discharge permits, aggregated by permit, joined to 303(d) impaired streams
partner_note: Mining's water impacts are a focus of WV Rivers Coalition and the FracTracker Alliance.
disclaimer: A permitted discharge is legal and treated; it is not proof of pollution. 'On an impaired stream' means a discharge outlet falls within ~800 m of a segment on WV's 2016 Clean Water Act 303(d) list — it flags where to look, not that this permit caused the impairment. See each permit's EPA ECHO effluent charts for measured discharge.
